blog.title: DeepSeek V4 API Migration Before July 24

long.title: How to Brief an AI Agent for Real Work That Gets Done

results.content:
DeepSeek aliases retire July 24. Migrate thinking and tool behavior—not just the name.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/deepseek-v4-api-migration-legacy-models/?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-19&utm_content=blog #DeepSeek

DeepSeek’s `deepseek-chat` and `deepseek-reasoner` aliases stop working after July 24 at 15:59 UTC. This guide maps each alias to an explicit V4 Flash thinking mode, adds a Flash-vs-Pro cost gate, and provides a migration test matrix for streaming, JSON, tools, and multi-turn state. Use it to cut over before the deadline without changing production behavior by accident.

DeepSeek’s legacy model aliases retire July 24, and changing the model name alone can silently change thinking behavior. This guide gives you a practical migration map, test matrix, canary plan, and rollback path.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/deepseek-v4-api-migration-legacy-models/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-19&utm_content=blog

DeepSeek aliases retire July 24. Map them to explicit V4 modes, then test thinking, tools, streaming, cost, and rollback before cutover.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/deepseek-v4-api-migration-legacy-models/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-19&utm_content=blog #DeepSeek #AIEngineering

DeepSeek’s legacy aliases retire July 24. Migrate behavior—not just names—with explicit modes, tool-loop tests, canaries, and rollback.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/deepseek-v4-api-migration-legacy-models/?utm_source=nostr&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-19&utm_content=blog

results.content:
Most “AI rewrites” fail because better was never defined. Use a generator, a separate evaluator, clear criteria, and a stopping rule.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vLdmWDaKRY #AIAgents

“Make it better” is not an evaluation criterion. This Tiny Agent short shows how evaluator-optimizer separates generation from judgment, turns feedback into measurable revision, and stops the loop before it drifts.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vLdmWDaKRY

If an AI keeps rewriting without getting clearly better, the missing piece is usually the evaluator. This short shows how to set criteria, separate generation from judgment, and define when the loop should stop.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vLdmWDaKRY

Vague feedback creates vague rewrites. Separate the generator from the evaluator, define measurable criteria, and set a stopping rule.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vLdmWDaKRY #AIAgents #LLM

Most AI rewrite loops fail because “better” was never defined. Use a separate evaluator, clear criteria, and a stopping rule.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2vLdmWDaKRY

results.content:
Don’t brief an agent from an empty prompt. Define the artifact, sources, constraints, review standard, destination, and evidence.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBCEk2pJFlo #AIAgents

Real agent work starts with a handoff, not an empty prompt box. This video turns the desired artifact, source pack, hard constraints, review standard, destination format, and evidence requirements into one reusable Agent Task Brief.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBCEk2pJFlo

Agents struggle when the task starts as a blank prompt instead of a real handoff. This video gives you a reusable brief for packaging the artifact, source material, constraints, review standard, destination, and proof of completion.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBCEk2pJFlo

A useful agent brief names the artifact, source pack, hard constraints, review standard, destination, and required evidence. This video turns those pieces into one reusable handoff.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBCEk2pJFlo #AIAgents #FutureOfWork

Don’t hand an agent a blank prompt. Package the artifact, sources, constraints, review standard, destination, and evidence into one task brief.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NBCEk2pJFlo
